title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Low Blood Pressure
intro: Herbal teas for Low blood pressure are a natural remedy that refers to a specific type of herbal tea designed to help alleviate symptoms of hypotension, or low blood pressure, a condition characterized by a blood pressure reading that is lower than normal. Herbal teas can treat this ailment because they contain various bioactive compounds that can help improve blood flow, increase energy levels, and strengthen the cardiovascular system. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat low blood pressure include Ginkgo biloba, which improves blood flow and circulation; Ginseng, which boosts energy and vitality; Dandelion root, which helps regulate blood pressure; Peppermint, which improves circulation and reduces inflammation; Ashwagandha, which helps reduce stress and anxiety; and Yarrow, which helps improve blood flow and circulation. By incorporating these herbal teas into a daily routine, individuals can help stabilize their blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypotension.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas, also known as Huangqi in traditional Chinese medicine, have been used for centuries to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic and cardiovascular-protective properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus membranaceus, including flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, help to increase blood flow and improve cardiovascular function, thus helping to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure. By enhancing the body's natural response to stress and promoting the production of red blood cells, Astragalus membranaceus teas help to increase blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. Regular consumption of Astragalus membranaceus teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating low blood pressure, including improved blood circulation, enhanced immune function, and reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it warm, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can cause potential side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset when used to treat low blood pressure, due to the increased blood flow and heart rate it stimulates. To minimize these side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid consuming Astragalus membranaceus teas before bedtime, and stay hydrated to prevent dehydration, especially in people with underlying kidney problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed and tolerance.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage accordingly every week.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized treatment plan.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their ability to improve circulation and increase blood flow to the extremities. This herbal preparation helps to treat low blood pressure by stimulating the heart and increasing the heart rate, thereby improving blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the tissues. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, play a crucial role in treating low blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving microcirculation. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat low blood pressure include improved blood circulation, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can find them at a health food store or online.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot. Let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba leaves to the cooled water. You can use a tea infuser or a muslin bag.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help raise blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "American journal of health-system pharmacy : AJHP : official journal of the American Society of Health-System Pharmacists", Ginkgo biloba teas have been studied in clinical trials for circulatory disturbances and may potentially help with low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate low blood pressure symptoms, but possible side effects include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to be cautious when consuming Ginkgo biloba teas with other medications or substances that may interact with its effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba for low blood pressure treatment.
Start with 1/2 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea, 2-3 times a day.
Gradually increase to 1 cup of tea, 3 times a day, as needed.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage or frequency as advised by doctor.
Continue treatment for 3-6 months to observe optimal results.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, including ginsenosides and panaxans, help to increase blood pressure by stimulating the release of stress hormones and improving cardiovascular function. By increasing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular function, Panax ginseng teas can help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure, such as dizziness and fatigue. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas has been shown to provide numerous benefits, including improved circulation, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced overall health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the dried Panax ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and add the measured honey (if using).
Drink the tea immediately and repeat as needed, ideally 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can help alleviate low blood pressure by stimulating blood circulation and increasing heart rate, but it may also cause side effects such as insomnia, anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and increased blood sugar levels due to its stimulating properties. To use Panax ginseng teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor your blood pressure regularly, and avoid consuming it before bedtime or when planning a low-intensity activity, as it can exacerbate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Panax ginseng tea for low blood pressure.
Drink 1 cup of Panax ginseng tea, 2-3 times a day, for 2-3 months.
Start with 1 teaspoon of ginseng per cup, adjust to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Monitor blood pressure regularly, adjusting dosage or frequency as needed.
Combine with other treatments if prescribed by your doctor for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve circulation. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, play a crucial role in stimulating the heart and increasing blood flow, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale teas also help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, further contributing to improved blood flow and pressure. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas may help to stabilize blood pressure and prevent dizziness, fatigue, and other symptoms associated with low blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Zingiber officinale root in a tea infuser or a small strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat low blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its active compounds interacting with the body's digestive system. Precautions when using Zingiber officinale teas for this purpose include taking small amounts and gradually increasing the dose to allow the body to adjust, as well as avoiding consumption on an empty stomach to minimize gastrointestinal discomfort.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for low blood pressure.
Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250-500 mg of dried Zingiber officinale per cup.
Gradually increase dosage to 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale per cup.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage as recommended by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their ability to increase blood pressure by stimulating the release of aldosterone, a hormone that helps regulate electrolyte balance. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and triterpenoids, help to constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure by blocking the action of 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme that converts cortisol into its inactive form. This herbal preparation helps to treat low blood pressure by increasing blood volume and cardiac output, leading to improved circulation and reduced symptoms of dizziness and fainting. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat low blood pressure include a natural and non-invasive approach, with minimal side effects and no interactions with other medications, making it a popular choice for those seeking alternative treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 2 cups of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the roots and chop them into small pieces. Add the chopped roots to the tea infuser or strainer.
Boil the 2 cups of water and pour it over the roots in the infuser or strainer. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the roots. Add honey or other sweetener to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week, to help manage low blood pressure. Consult a doctor before using as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat low blood pressure, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, electrolyte imbalances, and fluid retention due to its mineralocorticoid properties. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, monitor blood pressure regularly, and be cautious of excessive consumption which can lead to adverse effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for low blood pressure.
Drink 1/2 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with a low dose of 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of water.
Gradually increase the dose to 2 teaspoons per cup of water as needed.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage under doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their stimulating properties. The essential oils and bioactive constituents present in Cinnamomum verum teas, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, help to dilate blood vessels, improve circulation, and increase heart rate, thereby addressing the symptoms of low blood pressure. This herbal preparation works by increasing the production of red blood cells, improving oxygenation of tissues, and enhancing the body's ability to absorb and utilize nutrients, ultimately leading to an increase in blood pressure. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat low blood pressure include improved cardiovascular health, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 2 teaspoons of Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon cinnamon) bark in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Add the Ceylon cinnamon bark to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the cinnamon bark. Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to taste.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can help alleviate low blood pressure symptoms, but it may also cause side effects such as digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high cinnamon oil content. To use Cinnamomum verum teas safely, it's essential to consume them in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to liver damage, and to avoid taking them with other medications that lower blood pressure, as this may cause a sudden drop in blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor to confirm low blood pressure diagnosis and usage safety.
Take 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum tea powder in 1 cup of hot water.
Drink 1 cup, 2 times a day, for 1 week to observe blood pressure changes.
Increase to 2 cups a day if blood pressure improves, but not by more than 50%.
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust usage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used to treat low blood pressure due to their stimulating properties that help to increase heart rate and blood pressure. The Ma Huang plant, also known as Ephedra sinica, contains bioactive constituents such as ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by increasing blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. By stimulating the cardiovascular system, Ephedra sinica teas help to treat low blood pressure by improving circulation and increasing the heart's pumping efficiency. The benefits of using Ephedra sinica teas to treat low blood pressure include improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of dizziness and fainting episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ephedra sinica leaves.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and put it in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Ephedra sinica leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves or muslin bag.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can help alleviate low blood pressure, but using them may lead to side effects such as increased heart rate, high blood pressure, insomnia, anxiety, and stomach upset due to its stimulant properties. Precautions when using Ephedra sinica teas include being cautious with dosage, as excessive consumption may worsen symptoms, and avoiding use in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions, anxiety disorders, or other health issues.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ephedra sinica teas for low blood pressure.
Take 1 cup of Ephedra sinica tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Start with low dosage (250-500 mg per cup) and adjust as needed.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust dosage or frequency of use.
Continue using Ephedra sinica tea for 2-6 weeks, then reassess blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Rauvolfia serpentina teas, also known as Indian snakeroot, have been used in traditional medicine to treat low blood pressure or hypotension. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as ajmaline, reserpine, and yohimbine, which help to increase blood pressure by stimulating the heart and relaxing blood vessels. The properties of Rauvolfia serpentina teas, including their vasopressor and cardio-stimulant effects, help to treat low blood pressure by increasing cardiac output and peripheral resistance, thereby improving blood circulation and overall cardiovascular function. Regular consumption of Rauvolfia serpentina teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating low blood pressure, including improved blood pressure regulation, reduced risk of cardiovascular complications, and enhanced overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Rauvolfia serpentina dried root powder, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches boiling point, then reduce heat to low.
Add the dried root powder to the water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using the strainer and discard the root powder.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help manage low blood pressure, but consult a doctor before using it as a treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rauvolfia serpentina teas can cause various side effects when used to treat low blood pressure, including drowsiness, dizziness, and nausea, as well as potential interactions with other medications. To use Rauvolfia serpentina teas safely, it is recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and also to avoid consuming them in large quantities or for extended periods of time.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor or healthcare professional for advice on using Rauvolfia serpentina tea.
Start with 1/2 cup of Rauvolfia serpentina tea, 2-3 times a day.
Gradually increase to 1 cup of tea, 3-4 times a day, as advised by doctor.
Monitor blood pressure and adjust tea dosage and frequency as advised by doctor.
Continue using Rauvolfia serpentina tea for as long as advised by doctor or healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic and hemostatic properties, which help to regulate blood flow and maintain stable blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides, contribute to its therapeutic effects by improving blood circulation and reducing vascular resistance. By promoting vasodilation and increasing cardiac output, Schisandra chinensis teas can help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure, such as dizziness and fatigue. Regular consumption of Schisandra chinensis teas has been reported to be beneficial in treating low blood pressure, as it helps to maintain overall cardiovascular health and promote a sense of well-being.

Measure 2-3 grams of dried Schisandra chinensis and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Schisandra chinensis in the tea infuser or cup.

Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Schisandra chinensis per cup of hot water daily

Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup of hot water if needed
Monitor blood pressure regularly and adjust dosage as advised by your doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and promote balance in the cardiovascular system. This herbal preparation is particularly effective in treating low blood pressure as it helps to stimulate the nervous system and improve circulation, thereby increasing blood flow and oxygenation to the body's tissues. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to have a positive impact on blood pressure by increasing vasodilation and improving cardiac function. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat low blood pressure include reduced symptoms of dizziness and fatigue, improved overall circulation, and a more stable and balanced cardiovascular system.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 2-3 tablespoons of flowers for a single serving of tea.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the Echinacea flowers in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flowers to release their properties.
Strain the tea and drink it warm, 2-3 times a day, to help boost your blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause mild side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and stomach upset when used to treat low blood pressure, and in severe cases, it may interact with medications that lower blood pressure, leading to an excessive drop in blood pressure. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it is essential to monitor your blood pressure regularly and avoid consuming it in large quantities, especially if you have a history of heart problems or are taking medications that affect blood pressure.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas are a traditional herbal remedy used to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic properties, which help to balance the body's response to stress and promote overall well-being. This herbal preparation works by increasing the levels of blood pressure through the stimulation of the sympathetic nervous system, thereby counteracting the effects of hypotension. The bioactive constituents of Withania somnifera teas, including withanolides, withaferin A, and withanoside IV, have been shown to have vasopressor and cardiotonic effects, contributing to their ability to treat low blood pressure. The properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by increasing the cardiac output, reducing peripheral vascular resistance, and enhancing the sympathetic nervous system activity. The bioactive constituents of Cassia angustifolia, including anthraquinones and glycosides, play a crucial role in treating low blood pressure by modulating the cardiovascular system and improving the overall hemodynamic parameters. To use Cassia angustifolia teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and also to drink plenty of water to prevent dehydration and electrolyte imbalances.

The properties of this herbal preparation, including its vasodilatory and cardiotonic effects, help to increase blood flow and improve circulation, thus alleviating symptoms of low blood pressure. preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their properties that help to increase cardiac output and peripheral resistance. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as quinine and quinidine, help to stimulate the heart and improve blood circulation, thus addressing the underlying cause of hypotension. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas for low blood pressure include improved heart function, increased energy levels, and enhanced overall circulation, making it a natural remedy for individuals suffering from this condition. Regular consumption of Cinchona officinalis teas may also help to regulate blood pressure and prevent episodes of dizziness and fainting associated with low blood pressure.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona officinalis bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove the bark.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can help alleviate low blood pressure, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to the increased blood flow. Precautions should be taken when using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat low blood pressure, including monitoring blood pressure closely and avoiding consumption in individuals with a history of bleeding disorders or ulcers, as the increased blood flow may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas, also known as valerian root tea, have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and promote balance in the autonomic nervous system. The bioactive constituents of valerian root tea, including valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, work together to increase blood pressure by stimulating the sympathetic nervous system and improving blood flow. By consuming valerian root tea, individuals with low blood pressure can experience increased energy levels, improved circulation, and a reduction in symptoms such as dizziness and fainting. The benefits of valerian root tea in treating low blood pressure include its ability to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and provide a natural and non-addictive alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and place it in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Valeriana officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the Valeriana officinalis root. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of Valeriana officinalis tea 2-3 times a day to help manage low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas for low blood pressure may be beneficial due to the presence of alkaloids, including those that stimulate the body's activity, which is important for addressing hypotension.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can help alleviate low blood pressure symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, take it in moderation, start with a small dose, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery as its sedative effects can last for several hours.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure due to its unique properties that help to stimulate the cardiovascular system. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as avenacosides, avenanthramides, and ferulic acid, which have been found to help increase blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in blood vessels. Avena sativa teas work by stimulating the heart and increasing the production of red blood cells, which helps to improve circulation and increase blood pressure. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating low blood pressure, including reduced dizziness, improved energy levels, and enhanced overall cardiovascular health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa oats (you can buy this at a health food store or online).
Use a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to steep 2 tablespoons of Avena sativa in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help support low blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be an effective treatment for low blood pressure, but consuming it may also lead to side effects such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and fainting due to the increased blood pressure it induces. To avoid these risks, individuals should start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, as well as monitor their blood pressure closely, especially if they have pre-existing cardiovascular conditions.
